timeline.heading: Lineage of your questions
timeline.subheading: A record of how your questions have been born, grown, and shifted

hero.lead: Oryzae is a writing app inspired by koji mold — Aspergillus oryzae. Set a question that matters to you, write into it for a week, and the system decomposes your text, surfaces a few essential snippets, and returns a single short letter. Slow self-dialogue, made on purpose.
hero.foot: Vertical or horizontal writing, in Japanese, English, and beyond.

snippet.1: As a nukadoko slowly absorbs the household it lives in, an AI may also change its character…

hero.question: YOUR QUESTION

concept.title: Brew your question while you write.

p1.title: Set a question
p1.body: Capture, in one sentence, the question that matters most right now. Everything else ferments around it.

p2.title: Write daily
p2.body: On a quiet vertical page, write whatever comes — typos, detours and all. The trace of your hand stays.

p3.title: A letter arrives
p3.body: Each week, a short letter and a few keywords distilled from your own words come back. Read them, then begin again.

process.lead: Inside Oryzae, three microbes — like the inhabitants of a nukadoko — each take a different role, and together brew a week of your words.

koji.output: Output: an analysis worksheet, a result diagram, snippets that illuminate the question.

lactic.body: Working from the worksheet and the snippets, this stage traces how your thinking has moved this week — not as a summary, but as a roughly 500-character poetic note in vocabulary deliberately offset from your own.

yeast.body: Three to five short phrases — "the autonomy of the dead", "the dead activated by the living" — that step a little back from your question and widen the imaginative space around it.

preview.title: Open the places.
preview.lead: Write daily in the Editor. Arrange your week on the Board. Look back on it from above in the Jar. At week's end, a Letter arrives. Four places, one slow practice.

editor.title: Add a title
editor.caption: Editor — a quiet vertical page. Optional typing effects — drifting, ghosting, fading characters — bring a small body to keystrokes.

jar.label: YOUR QUESTION · What is a design that lets nature inhabit the body?

letter.body: You have walked, this week, around the question of how the dead and the living might continue to live together. In a dream, the figure of Y arrives in ordinary clothes, then shrinks — quietly — back into the shape of a foetus and disappears. It is loss, and it is also a return to origin, a faint outline of a circular view of life and death. Across the week you begin to notice that an analytical posture may not be the right one toward what matters most. Forgetting, perhaps, is one form of living-with.
letter.caption: Letter — a 500-character letter, weekly. Not a summary; a small ember to keep you writing.

snippet.1: Forgetting, perhaps, is one form of living-with.
snippet.2: On nukadoko, people, and the way time mixes in.

entry.title: A design that feels the mountain

board.label: 7 cards · 5 entries · 2 snippets · 3 photos
board.caption: Board — A daily and weekly canvas where entries, snippets and photos sit beside each other. Memories begin to settle through relations, not through time.

2.title: Without immediacy
2.body: AI replies are deliberately delayed — once a week, like a letter. The exchange shifts from information processing to a quiet kind of relationship.

3.title: Fermentative time
3.body: Not an answer, a brewing. As a nukadoko takes on the flavour of the household it lives in, your thinking absorbs your own time and microbiome and slowly ripens.

4.title: Tech you can outgrow
4.body: The point is not dependence. Oryzae is meant to support, then quietly fall away as your own self-dialogue gets stronger.

lineage.label: LINEAGE
lineage.body: Oryzae carries forward what we learned from Pickles (2020–), a journaling tool for self-dialogue. The wider research context lives at ephemere.io.

1.q: How is this different from Pickles?
1.a: Pickles was a place to write the journal itself. Oryzae sets a question at the centre and adds the time around the writing — a fermentation process that returns your own words to you in a different shape.

2.q: Do I have to write vertically?
2.a: No. Vertical writing is the default, but you can switch to horizontal in one click. The font flips between mincho (serif) and gothic (sans) too.

3.a: Only the question you set, and the entries you tied to it during that week. Anything not tied to a question stays out of the fermentation.

4.q: Can I write in languages other than Japanese?
4.a: Yes — multilingual input is supported, including English. Vertical writing is tuned for Japanese and Chinese.

5.q: What stage is the project at?
5.a: Oryzae is a research preview. The shape of the app and its behaviour will continue to shift alongside the research.

step_concept.body: Plant a question on your mind, then water it with daily entries. Over time, an AI ferments what you've written and returns it as new insight.
step_concept.body_2: It works a little differently from a regular diary — let's walk through the four places that make it tick.

step_question.title: Plant your first question.
step_question.body: Try shaping what you're most preoccupied with into a short question. "Why…", "How might I…" — a sentence whose answer you don't have yet is plenty. You can edit it any time.
step_question.placeholder: e.g. Why am I bad at hurrying?
step_question.hint: It will be stored in the Jar — fermentation starts here.

step_editor.body_before_em: The editor is a quiet place to write. Put down whatever's on your mind. Select a sentence to lift it out as a
step_editor.body_after_em: — you can lay them out side by side later on the Board.

step_ferment.title: Let your questions rest in the Jar.
step_ferment.body: The entries you collect settle into the Jar and ferment on a schedule. The AI re-reads your words, finds patterns and shifts in your questions, and offers them back gently — that's the rhythm of Oryzae.

metadata.title: Support
metadata.description: How to use Oryzae, and answers to frequent questions.

hero.title: How to use Oryzae
hero.lead: Small things you might wonder about while living with Oryzae, gathered in one place — including the small details we don't cover on the landing page or in the welcome tour.

intro.title: Oryzae is made of four places.
intro.lead: Oryzae is a writing app that turns around a single question. You set a question, write entries into it, jar them, wait for fermentation, then read a letter. Four places carry that rhythm.

place_editor.name: Editor
place_editor.body: A quiet, vertical (or horizontal) page for daily writing. Optional typing effects bring a small body to each keystroke.

place_jar.name: Jar
place_jar.body: Where your question lives, together with the entries linked to it. Both the place fermentation prepares in, and a way to look at the week from above.

place_board.name: Board
place_board.body: A flat surface for laying out entries, snippets, and photos. Daily and weekly modes — memory takes shape through relations rather than time.

place_letter.name: Letter
place_letter.body: Each weekend, a 500-character letter and 3–5 keywords arrive — distilled by koji, lactic, and yeast. Not a summary; a small ember to keep you writing.

basics.title: The basic rhythm
basics.lead: A typical week with Oryzae looks roughly like this.

step_1.title: 1. Set a question
step_1.body: Create one from the header or the Jar. It doesn't have to be polished — "Why…", "How might I…", a sentence whose answer you don't yet have, is plenty. You can edit it any time.

step_2.title: 2. Write entries
step_2.body: Write in the editor whenever you like. Titles are optional. Entries auto-save every few seconds; the status bar shows editing / saving / saved.

step_3.title: 3. Tie an entry to your question (pickle it)
step_3.body: On save, choose "pickle" to link the entry to your current question. Entries that aren't tied to a question stay out of the Jar — and out of fermentation.

step_4.title: 4. Wait for fermentation
step_4.body: On the weekend, the week's entries that are tied to your question pass through three stages — koji decomposes, lactic writes a letter, yeast offers keywords. Replies are deliberately delayed; that is the rhythm Oryzae aims for.

step_5.title: 5. Read the letter and keywords
step_5.body: When fermentation completes, you can open a 500-character letter and 3–5 keywords from the Jar. Not a summary — a comment written deliberately a little off from your own vocabulary, to keep your thinking moving.

step_6.title: 6. Look back from the Board
step_6.body: On the Board, the week's entries, snippets, and photos sit side by side. Daily and weekly views let you move between a single day's density and the whole week's flow.

editor.title: Editor essentials
editor.lead: The editor is the heart of Oryzae. A few small features take care of the act of writing itself.

feature_layout.title: Vertical / horizontal
feature_layout.body: Switch between vertical and horizontal writing from the toolbar at any time. Default is vertical, tuned for Japanese and Chinese.

feature_font.title: Mincho / Gothic
feature_font.body: Toggle between mincho (serif, Noto Serif JP) and gothic (sans, Noto Sans JP) when you want to change the breath of the page.

feature_autosave.title: Auto-save
feature_autosave.body: Saves automatically every few seconds. The status — editing, saving, saved — sits in the status bar.

feature_snippet.title: Snippets
feature_snippet.body: Select a sentence in the body and a "snippet" button appears. Lift up to 50 characters out as a snippet, to lay them next to others on the Board later.

feature_link_question.title: Linking to a question
feature_link_question.body: On save, you can link the entry to your current question. Only linked entries enter the Jar and become candidates for fermentation.

feature_stats.title: Writing stats
feature_stats.body: Open the stats panel from the toolbar for character / line / paragraph counts and an estimated reading time.

feature_voice.title: Voice input
feature_voice.body: Toggle voice input from the microphone icon in the toolbar. Chrome is recommended. Microphone permission is required.

feature_fullscreen.title: Fullscreen
feature_fullscreen.body: When you want everything else out of view. Click again to return to the normal layout.

effects.title: Editor effects
effects.lead: Small effects that bring a touch of body to the act of writing. Each one can be toggled individually from the settings icon at the top right of the editor.

row_focus.name: Focus mode
row_focus.what: Dims everything except the paragraph the cursor is in.
row_focus.when: Sinking into a single paragraph at a time.

row_time.name: Time Inscription
row_time.what: Encodes per-key dwell time and speed into character size, weight, and pressure bleed.
row_time.when: Leaving rhythm and emphasis on the page itself.

row_eraser.name: Eraser Trace
row_eraser.what: Deleted characters fade out gradually instead of disappearing instantly.
row_eraser.when: Looking back at the path of revision.

row_amp.name: AMP / ASMR
row_amp.what: Picks up keystroke sounds via the mic and re-amplifies them through filters and a compressor.
row_amp.when: Days when you simply love the sound of writing. Mic permission required.

row_voice.name: Voice Dynamics
row_voice.what: Reflects the volume of your voice, picked up by the mic, into character weight and size.
row_voice.when: Speaking aloud while you write or think. Mic permission required.

row_ghost.name: Ghost
row_ghost.what: Characters leave a soft afterimage that blurs and fades. Two modes: block (on commit) and dust (per key).
row_ghost.when: Watching the rhythm of writing itself.

effects.outro: Size, scatter, blur, and duration can all be tuned from the settings panel.

account.title: Account & settings
account.lead: From the Account page, you can change the following.

item_nickname.title: Nickname
item_nickname.body: The name shown inside the app. It doesn't have to be your real name.

item_email.title: Email
item_email.body: A confirmation email is sent to the new address; clicking the link there finishes the change. Accounts using Google sign-in only can't change the email here.

item_password.title: Password
item_password.body: Both the current and a new password (6+ characters) are required. Not shown for accounts that use only Google sign-in.

item_language.title: Language
item_language.body: Switch between Japanese and English. The setting follows the account across devices.

item_stats.title: Writing stats
item_stats.body: From the Account page, see your total entries and total characters written so far.

1.q: Where is what I write stored?
1.a: It is sent to Oryzae's server over SSL and stored in an encrypted database (Supabase). No-one but your authenticated account can read it.

2.a: Only the question you set, and the entries you have tied to that question. Drafts and casual notes that aren't tied to a question stay out of fermentation.

3.a: Roughly on the weekend, against the entries linked to your question that week. Fermentation may be skipped depending on entry volume and a small time gate.

4.q: How do I delete an entry?
4.a: Open the entry from the list and choose delete from the menu. Even if it was linked to a question, deleting it removes it from fermentation as well.

5.q: How many questions can I have?
5.a: You can keep several questions in the Jar at once. We recommend choosing one as the "current question" and tying entries to that, so the fermentation has a clear focus.

6.q: Do I have to write vertically?
6.a: No. Vertical is the default, but you can switch to horizontal from the toolbar at any time. The font flips between mincho (serif) and gothic (sans) too.

7.q: Can I write in languages other than Japanese?
7.a: Yes — multilingual input is supported, including English. Fermentation responds in the language you write in. Vertical writing is tuned for Japanese and Chinese.

8.q: Voice input or mic-based effects don't work
8.a: Voice input, AMP / ASMR, and Voice Dynamics all need microphone access. We recommend Chrome. Please check your OS / browser microphone permissions, including the per-site permission for this app.

9.q: Can I use Oryzae on my phone?
9.a: You can read and jot down short notes, but Oryzae is designed for sitting down and writing on a desktop. To get the most out of vertical writing, the effects, and the stats, desktop Chrome is recommended.

10.a: Oryzae is currently a research preview. Pricing has not been set, and use is free. Any future changes will be announced on the landing page and by email.

11.q: How is Oryzae different from Pickles?
11.a: Pickles was a place to write the journal itself. Oryzae sets a question at the centre and adds the time around the writing — a fermentation process that returns your own words to you in a different shape. Data migration from Pickles is not currently offered.

12.q: How do I delete my account?
12.a: For now, account deletion is handled manually. Please get in touch via the contact below. The same goes for exporting data you've written so far.

contact.title: Contact
contact.lead: If something is broken, you have a request, or a phrase here is misleading, please write in. We read every message.
contact.note: Oryzae is a research preview. Behaviour and design will continue to shift alongside the research.

outro.lead: Even on a day you can't write, there's always tomorrow.
